title: Bloodbath After Jobs Report — $390B Wiped From Crypto
summary: A hotter-than-expected May jobs report (139K vs 130K) triggered a brutal selloff across crypto and equities. ~$390B was wiped from the crypto market, with ETH collapsing 51% from January highs. The S&P 500 fell 2.6% and the 10Y yield spiked to 4.51%. Fear & Greed Index hit 8 — Extreme Fear.

stories.title: Jobs Report Triggers Selloff
stories.content: May payrolls came in at 139K vs 130K expected, reinforcing the "higher for longer" Fed narrative. Markets sold off sharply on the repricing of rate-cut expectations. The strong labor market gives the Fed no urgency to cut.

stories.title: Bitcoin Drops to $61,400
stories.content: Bitcoin fell to ~$61,400, down ~5% on the day. BTC dominance surged to 64% as altcoins got hit even harder. Nearly $1B in leveraged positions were liquidated across crypto derivatives markets.

stories.title: Ethereum Collapses 51% From January Highs
stories.content: ETH dropped to ~$1,584, a 51% decline from its January peak. The ratio against BTC continued to deteriorate. DeFi TVL declined alongside, and on-chain activity showed capitulation-level outflows from exchanges.

stories.title: US Spot Bitcoin ETFs See $616M in Outflows
stories.content: Spot Bitcoin ETFs recorded $616M in net outflows — the largest single-day outflow in weeks. This suggests institutional risk-off behavior, not just retail panic. BlackRock's IBIT still holds the bulk of AUM but flows turned sharply negative.

stories.title: Strategy Buys Another 1,045 BTC
stories.content: Strategy (formerly MicroStrategy) purchased an additional 1,045 BTC (~$64M), continuing its aggressive accumulation strategy. They now hold over 580,000 BTC. A contrarian bullish signal amid the panic.

stories.title: Gold Holds $3,310, DXY ~99.1
stories.content: Gold held near $3,310 as a safe-haven bid emerged. The DXY was steady around 99.1. Traditional safe havens weren't immune — gold was flat rather than surging, suggesting broad deleveraging rather than a flight to safety.

stories.title: US Regulatory Clarity Continues to Build
stories.content: Despite the selloff, regulatory progress continued. The SEC dropped cases against Ripple and Coinbase. The GENIUS Act (stablecoin legislation) passed the Senate. Wyoming launched its state-backed stable token. The regulatory environment is structurally improving.

stories.title: Macro Outlook: Higher for Longer
stories.content: The strong jobs report reinforced the narrative that the Fed will hold rates at 4.50-4.75% through summer. Tariff risks (Trump's latest threats) and Tesla's 14% drop on the Musk-Trump feud added to the risk-off tone. Key week ahead: FOMC on June 18.
